theoremName: exact and strong-limit branch transport theorem

exactUCriterionStatus.label: exact branch transport criterion
exactUCriterionStatus.reason: The exact U problem is reduced to equality of the joint Hardy and Volterra/KLM feature Gram kernels.  This is the standard Hilbert-space isometry extension criterion.

exactUConstructedStatus.label: exact branch transport U constructed
exactUConstructedStatus.reason: The criterion is known, but no concrete z -> x_z map into the completed Volterra trace image has been constructed.
exactUConstructedStatus.blocker: z -> x_z in the completed Volterra trace image, with G_±(z)=G_±(f_{x_z})

hardyStrongTruncationStatus.label: Hardy branch strong truncation
hardyStrongTruncationStatus.reason: h_{z,R}^±=1_[0,R]h_z^± converges strongly to h_z^± in L^2(0,infty).  The finite signed Gram error is explicitly bounded by the exponential tail displayed in this JSON.

klmClosedConeLimitStatus.label: KLM closed-cone branch limit
klmClosedConeLimitStatus.reason: The Hardy truncation limit is closed, but the truncated Hardy branches have not yet been represented as KLM pullbacks or strong limits of KLM/Volterra feature branches.
klmClosedConeLimitStatus.blocker: Construct x_{z,R} or phase-space pullback vectors such that G_±(x_{z,R}) -> h_{z,R}^± jointly, with Gram convergence.

exactTransportCriterion.statement: A single isometry U with U h_z^+=G_+(z) and U h_z^-=G_-(z) exists on the finite evaluation span iff the joint Gram matrix of {h_z^+,h_z^-} equals the joint Gram matrix of {G_+(z),G_-(z)}. The continuum statement is the corresponding Kolmogorov/isometry extension after density.
exactTransportCriterion.missingConcreteMap: z -> x_z in the completed Volterra trace image, with G_±(z)=G_±(f_{x_z})

nextProofTarget: Construct the concrete evaluation trace map z -> x_z, or truncated maps x_{z,R}, so that the Volterra/KLM branch features have the same joint Gram limits as the Hardy branches.
